food_subgroup: Steamed filled buns (baozi) with pork filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general steamed bun class (FOODON_03302050); no specific pork-filled baozi entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers steamed bread and buns broadly; no pork-filled baozi-specific code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for steamed pork buns or baozi. Null assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 6 steamed pork buns (approximately 360 g total, ~60 g per bun), as sold frozen by Din Tai Fung at Marina Bay Link Mall, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Steamed Pork Bun (Baozi) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo data for pork-filled steamed buns
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (FoodData Central) and Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA entry for frozen steamed pork buns exists. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for 'Steamed bun with pork filling' (NDB 18350 / analogous composite) and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient data for pork-filled baozi. Per-100g values represent the combined dough-and-filling product as consumed after steaming. Macros are consistent with published ranges for baozi (wheat-flour dough ~55% carbohydrate, pork filling contributing protein and fat).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of steamed wheat-flour buns with pork filling — analogous estimate based on steamed white bread / baozi GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for frozen steamed pork buns (baozi) was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 45 is estimated from the closest analogous food: steamed white-flour buns (baozi, plain or with filling) reported in the University of Sydney GI Database with GI values in the range 40–50 (mean ~45). The pork filling lowers the effective GI relative to plain steamed bread (GI ~65) due to protein and fat content slowing gastric emptying. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 110.0) / 100 = 49.5, rounded to 49.7 accounting for 360 g serving with 30.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g (total carbohydrate ~109.8 g).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1512 mg per 6-bun serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of steamed pork buns due to the high sodium content (~1512 mg per 6-piece serving, representing approximately 66% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg), and should consider reducing portion size to 2–3 buns per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~45) and high total carbohydrate load per serving (~110 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although the glycemic index of steamed pork buns is moderate-low (~45), the large carbohydrate load per standard 6-piece serving (~110 g carbohydrate) produces a high glycemic load (~50), and individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should restrict portion size to 2–3 buns and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.3 g per 100 g) and sodium (~420 mg per 100 g) from pork filling
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Steamed pork buns contain moderate saturated fat (~8.3 g per 6-piece serving) and high sodium (~1512 mg per serving); individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake to 2–3 buns per occasion and choose lower-sodium dipping sauces to reduce overall cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed pork buns (鲜肉包, xiān ròu bāo), commonly known as baozi or pork bao, are a classic Chinese dim sum and everyday staple consisting of a soft, leavened wheat-flour dough wrapper encasing a seasoned minced pork filling. The filling typically contains ground pork, soy sauce, sesame oil, ginger, spring onion, and a small amount of sugar, producing a savory, slightly sweet, and aromatic interior. The buns are steamed until the dough is pillowy, moist, and slightly translucent at the base, with a characteristic smooth dome. This frozen version, sold by Din Tai Fung at Marina Bay Link Mall in Singapore, is produced to restaurant quality and requires home steaming before consumption. Per 100 g, the product provides approximately 218 kcal, 9.5 g protein, 6.8 g fat (2.3 g saturated), and 30.5 g carbohydrate, with a notable sodium content of ~420 mg. The glycemic index is estimated at ~45 (low-moderate), but the glycemic load per standard 6-piece serving is high (~50) due to the large carbohydrate mass. Clinically, the high sodium content is the primary concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management, while the high carbohydrate load per serving warrants portion control for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. Steamed pork buns are a culturally significant food across Chinese-speaking communities in Singapore, China, Taiwan, and the broader Chinese diaspora, commonly consumed at breakfast, brunch, or as a snack.

confidence_reason: No exact USDA or HPB Foodo entry for frozen steamed pork buns from Din Tai Fung exists. Nutrient values are adapted from the closest composite analogues (USDA steamed bun with pork filling and HPB Foodo baozi data). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for steamed wheat-flour buns. Confidence is medium rather than low because the food type is well-characterised in the literature and the adapted values are internally consistent and plausible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central composite data for steamed bun with pork filling and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore Foodo data for pork-filled baozi; no exact branded entry for Din Tai Fung frozen steamed pork buns was available in any approved source. 2. Serving size of 360 g (6 × ~60 g buns) is based on the field survey listing of 6 pieces at SGD 17.03 and typical per-bun weight for Din Tai Fung-style baozi. 3. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for steamed wheat-flour buns with filling; no direct measurement for this specific product was found. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 5. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 6. trans_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 7. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 8. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 9. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 10. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 11. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 12. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 13. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source composite. 14. The product contains pork and is therefore not halal or kosher. 15.
